zh.lifeSummary: 當John V Sir Savage於1370年出生在英國英格蘭爾克里夫頓時，他的父親John Savage36歲，母親Margaret d'Anyers18歲。他於1401年7月之前與Maud Swynnerton結婚。他們至少是6個兒子和5個女兒的父母。他去世於1450年8月1日在英國爾克里夫頓享年80歲，埋葬在法國i萊-德-福朗斯豪-德-布洛-比藍古特。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当John V Sir Savage于1370年出生在英国英格兰爾克里夫頓时，他的父亲John Savage36岁，母亲Margaret d'Anyers18岁。他于1401年7月之前与Maud Swynnerton结婚。他们至少是6个儿子和5个女儿的父母。他去世于1450年8月1日在英国爾克里夫頓享年80岁，埋葬在法国i萊-德-福朗斯豪-德-布洛-比藍古特。

nameMeanings.name: Sir
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: German: variant of   Sier  , unexplained. This surname is very rare in Germany.
Czech (Šír): nickname from Middle High German schīr ‘sheer, pure, clean’. Compare Schier .

variants.name: Sier
meanings: Dutch and German: from a short form of the personal name Sierik, Sierich, composed of the ancient Germanic elements sigi ‘victory’ + rīc ‘power(ful)’, or from a short form of the personal name Sigihard, composed of the ancient Germanic elements sigi + hari ‘army’.
English: variant of   Syer  .

nameMeanings.name: Savage
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: of Norman origin, a nickname for a wild or uncouth person, from Middle English and Old French salvage, sauvage ‘wild, untamed’ (from Late Latin salvaticus, literally ‘man of the woods’, a derivative of Latin silva ‘wood’ influenced by Latin salvus ‘whole’, i.e. natural). Compare French Sauvage .

Irish (Down): generally of English origin (it was taken to County Down in the 12th century), this name has also sometimes been adopted as equivalent of Gaelic Ó Sabháin, the name of a small south Munster sept, which was earlier Anglicized as O'Savin (see Savin ).
Americanized form of Jewish (Ashkenazic) Savich and of Serbian Savić (see Savic ).

variants.name: Savitt
meanings: Possibly English or Irish: variant of   Savage  . This surname is very rare in Britain.
Alternatively, perhaps an Americanized form of Jewish   Savich   or an American shortened and altered form of Jewish   Savitski  .

variants.name: Sauvage
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Michel, Armand, Jean-Jacques.

French and Channel Islands: nickname for a wild or uncouth person, from Old French salvage, sauvage ‘untamed’ (from Late Latin salvaticus, a derivative of Latin silva ‘wood’, influenced by Latin salvus ‘whole’, i.e. natural). This surname is also found in England.
History: Jean Sauvage from Plouëc-du-Trieux in Côtes-d'Armor, Brittany (France), married Marguerite Martin in Les Cèdres, QC, in 1782.

variants.name: Salvage
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Andre, Monique.
English (Sussex): variant of   Savage  .
Americanized form of some similar (like-sounding) Jewish surname.

variants.name: Ramage
meanings: Scottish and English (of Norman origin); French: nickname for a savage or unpredictable individual, from Old French ramage ‘wild, untamed’. The word is recorded once in Middle English with the sense ‘courageous’ and in Scots (spelled rammage) as ‘rash, furious’. Compare Savage .

variants.name: Sabin
meanings: English (Warwickshire and Northamptonshire): from the Middle English personal name Sabin (from Latin Sabinus; see 2 below) or its female equivalent Sabine (from Latin Sabina). In medieval England the feminine form was always more popular as a personal name.

French: from the Old French personal name Sabin, from Latin Sabinus. The name was originally referred to the Sabines, an ancient Italic people of central Italy whose name is of uncertain origin. In the 8th century BC the Romans slaughtered the Sabine menfolk and carried off the women. More influential as far as name-giving is concerned was the existence of several early Christian saints named Sabinus.
Irish: shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Sabháin ‘descendant of Sabhán’, a personal name based on sabh ‘cub’. As an Irish surname, this has mostly been absorbed into Savage .

variants.name: Selvidge
meanings: English (Leicestershire):
from Middle English selvage, silvadge ‘edge of a cloth, bandage, ornamental edging (to a cloth or garment)’, hence a nickname for someone who made or sold such items.
variant of   Savage  .

variants.name: Savidge
meanings: English: variant of   Savage  .

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When John V Sir Savage was born in 1370, in Clifton, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom, his father, John Savage, was 36 and his mother, Margaret d'Anyers, was 18. He married Maud Swynnerton before July 1401. They were the parents of at least 6 sons and 5 daughters. He died on 1 August 1450, in Clifton, Cheshire, England, at the age of 80, and was buried in Boulogne-Billancourt, Hauts-de-Seine, Île-de-France, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque John V Sir Savage est né en 1370, à Clifton, Cheshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, son père, John Savage, avait 36 ans et sa mère, Margaret d'Anyers, avait 18 ans. Il a épousé Maud Swynnerton avant juillet 1401. Ils ont eu au moins 6 fils et 5 filles. Il est décédé le 1 août 1450, à Clifton, Cheshire, Angleterre, à l’âge de 80 ans, et a été enterré à Boulogne-Billancourt, Hauts-de-Seine, Île-de-France, France.

de.lifeSummary: Als John V Sir Savage 1370 in Clifton, Cheshire, England, United Kingdom geboren wurde, war sein Vater John Savage 36 und seine Mutter Margaret d'Anyers 18. Er hat vor Juli 1401 Maud Swynnerton geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 6 Söhne und 5 Töchter. Er starb am 1. August 1450 im Alter von 80 Jahren in Clifton, Cheshire, England und wurde in Boulogne-Billancourt, Hauts-de-Seine, Île-de-France, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Quando John V Sir Savage è nato il nel 1370 nel Clifton, Cheshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, il suo padre, John Savage, aveva 36 anni e il suo madre, Margaret d'Anyers, aveva 18 anni. Lui sposato Maud Swynnerton prima del luglio 1401. Erano genitori di almeno 6 figli e 5 figlie. Morì il 1 agosto 1450, nel Clifton, Cheshire, England, all'età di 80 anni, e fu sepolto a Boulogne-Billancourt, Hauts-de-Seine, Île-de-France, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: John V Sir Savageが1370年にイギリスイギリスチェシュアイアクリフトンで生まれた時，父John Savageは36歳，母Margaret d'Anyersは18歳であった。1401年7月以前にMaud Swynnertonと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも6人の息子と5人の娘の親であった。彼は1450年8月1日にイギリスチェシュアイアクリフトンで80歳で死去し，フランスiレ-デ-フランスハウトス-デ-セアインボウログネ-ビランコウルトにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: John V Sir Savage(이)가 1370년 영국 잉글랜드 체실르 클리프턴에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 John Savage(은)는 36세였고, 그의 어머니 Margaret d'Anyers(은)는 18세였다. 그는 1401년 7월 이전 Maud Swynnerton(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 여섯 명의 아들과 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1450년 8월 1일 영국 체실르 클리프턴에서 80세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 i레-데-프랜스 하츠-데-세이인 부로그니-빌랭쿠르트에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando John V Sir Savage nasceu em 1370, em Clifton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, seu pai, John Savage, tinha 36 anos e sua mãe, Margaret d'Anyers, tinha 18 anos. Ele casou-se com Maud Swynnerton antes julho de 1401. Eles tiveram pelo menos 6 filhos e 5 filhas. Ele faleceu em 1 de agosto de 1450, em Clifton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, com 80 anos, e foi sepultado em Boulogne-Billancourt, Hauts-de-Seine, Île-de-France, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда John V Sir Savage родился в 1370 г. в местности клифтон, чешир, Англия, Великобритания, его отцу по имени John Savage было 36 лет, а матери, которую звали Margaret d'Anyers, 18. Он женился на женщине по имени Maud Swynnerton перед июля 1401 г. У них было по крайней мере 6 сыновей и 5 дочерей. Он умер 1 августа 1450 г. в местности клифтон, чешир, Англия в возрасте 80 лет и был похоронен в местности боулогне-билланкоурт, хауц-де-син, iле-де-фрэнс, Франция.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando John V Sir Savage nació en 1370, en Clifton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, su papá, John Savage, tenía 36 años y su mamá, Margaret d'Anyers, tenía 18 años. Se casó con Maud Swynnerton antes de julio de 1401. Fueron padres de por lo menos 6 hijos y 5 hijas. Murió el 1 de agosto de 1450, en Clifton, Cheshire, Inglaterra, a la edad de 80 años, y fue sepultado en Boulogne-Billancourt, Hauts-de-Seine, Île-de-France, Francia.
